sutta: and 30 8 the buddha said monks these 4 persons are found in the world what for the subdued in body but not in mind the unsubdued in body but subdued in mind he who is subdued in neither body nor mind he who is subdued in both body and mind and how monks is a person subdued in body but not in mind in this case a certain person makes his bed and lodging in the lonely glades and solitude of a forest but meanwhile things sensual thoughts malicious thoughts and harmful thoughts thus monks a person is subdued in body but not in mind and how is a person unsubdued in body but subdued in mind in this case a certain person makes not his bed and lodging in the forest yet meanwhile he thinks unworldly thoughts thoughts not malicious not harmful thus is a person unsubdued in body but subdued in mind and how is a person subdued neither in body nor in mind in this case a certain person makes not his bid and lodging in the forest that mean while he thinks sensual thoughts malicious thoughts and harmful thoughts and how is a person subdued both in body and in mind in this case a certain person makes his bed and lodging in the lonely glaze and solitude of a forest and at the same time things unworldly thoughts thoughts not malicious not harmful thus is a person subdued both in body and in mind so these were 4 persons are found existing in the world and this is about viveka viveka means seclusion and there are 2 types of seclusion body seclusion kaya viveka and seclusion of the mind chitta viveka and um kaya viveka is the person who is secluded in the body that means he goes in he isolates himself from other people and he goes to the forest but uh the first case is the person who has a body seclusion but he has no seclusion of the mind he still has a myth his mid is sensual thoughts all the wrong thoughts like sensual thoughts malicious thoughts and harmful thoughts so this is a type of person for example 1 who is new on the spiritual path who his mind is not very well developed he goes to the forest and meanwhile he carries with him all these wrong thoughts and it takes some time for him to subdue his wrong thoughts but if he persists he would probably be able to subdue his these wrong thoughts the second case is a person who has chitta viveka but not kaya viveka he does not go into physical seclusion and yet he has quite a developed mind he does not think sensual thoughts or malicious thoughts or harmful thoughts uh so this is a kind of person whose mind is more developed somebody probably who has cultivated himself in a previous lifetime so even as a lay person he already has a developed mind the third person is 1 who is who does who has neither body seclusion nor but nor seclusion of the mind and that is a very ordinary person very worldly person and then the fourth 1 who is subdued both in body and mind who has both seclusion a body and mind and this is the best because the buddha always encourages among to be secluded both in body and mind with that that would be the ideal conditions for progress in the spiritual path in the holy life but of course later even among uh necessary he can always come back to be with people so that he can impart his knowledge of the dharma etc now we come to
